title: allium sativum f. pekinense
intro: Allium sativum f. pekinense, also known as Chinese garlic, is a variety of garlic that is native to China. It is a type of plant that belongs to the Allium genus, which is known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. Chinese garlic is rich in compounds that have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which can help to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. It can be consumed as raw garlic, used in cooking, or prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of tea, capsules, or infusions, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine.
